title: Reduced average length of stay salvaged hospitals' operating margins in March: report
summary: Hospitals' average length of stay declined 2% from February to March, fueling a 4% month-to-month decline in total daily calendar expense that outpaced a 1% dip in total daily revenue, according to healthcare advisory services firm Kaufman Hall.

title: Tenet, Leapfrog spar over legal fees in hospital ratings case
summary: Tenet wants Leapfrog to pay $10.5 million in legal fees after a judge agreed that the nonprofit unfairly deflated Tenet’s hospital ratings. The penalty would “cast a specter of financial ruin” over the nonprofit, Leapfrog said.

title: U.S. hospital shootings have become more common over past 25 years: Mayo Clinic study
summary: Between 2000 and 2024, hospital-based shootings increased from six to 34 per year—or a 6.4% increase per year. And, from 2012 to 2024, incidents rose 8.4% year-over-year from 14 to 34 shootings.
